ogTitle: Burnett Creek vs Burnett Heads: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Burnett Creek and Burnett Heads.
heading: Burnett Creek vs Burnett Heads — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Burnett Creek, Queensland and Burnett Heads, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Burnett Creek has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Burnett Heads (33/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Burnett Creek or Burnett Heads safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Burnett Creek has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Burnett Heads scores 51/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Burnett Creek scores 26/100 for bushfire risk. Burnett Heads scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Burnett Creek or Burnett Heads?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Burnett Creek has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.4% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
